persona.the_one_thing: Dongwon is a former data scientist / AI research fellow turned VC who just landed his first exit (Quantinuum) on an openly contrarian deep-tech bet — so walk in leading with the actual architecture and reasoning rigor of Orbt's Claude-native agents plus a defensible 'why others are wrong' thesis, not buzzwords. Confidence: high (sourced to his own posts s27/s32 + career record s2).
persona.snapshot: Dongwon Sung (also appears as 'Dongwon Ellie Sung') is a Principal at Korea Investment Partners (KIP), the ~$4B-AUM venture arm of Korea Investment Holdings, covering AI & Digital. He reached investing through a hands-on technical path — Data Scientist at SK hynix and then AI Research Fellow at SK innovation — before joining KIP in April 2022. His public posting footprint skews heavily deep-tech: quantum (Quantinuum, QEDMA), edge/on-device AI (ENERZAi), manufacturing AI (INTERX) and AI inference infrastructure (NeuReality).
persona.whats_new: In June 2026 he publicly marked his first exit — Quantinuum's public listing — framing it as a vindicated contrarian quantum bet (s27). Earlier in 2026 he was a featured speaker at a Microsoft × KIP 'Synergy Club' session on global AI trends and how startups scale globally (s32), and has been actively amplifying his deep-tech/AI portfolio.

recent_activity.what: Posted about Quantinuum's public listing — called it his 'first exit' and a vindicated contrarian quantum bet
Slated to speak at Microsoft × KIP 'Synergy Club' on global AI trends and how startups scale globally
Amplified ENERZAi's 'AI on Edge' on-device AI seminar (already-commercialized ultra-light models)
Amplified INTERX passing KOSDAQ tech evaluation — manufacturing/autonomous-manufacturing AI, 200+ use cases
Amplified NeuReality's 'token factory OS' AI-inference infrastructure post
Congratulated QEDMA's Asif Sinay on the Q-Net Award ('proud to be part of the journey')

top_moves.trait: Ex-technologist who will probe the build
top_moves.fact: Worked as Data Scientist at SK hynix (2018–2020) and AI Research Fellow at SK innovation (2020–2022) before becoming a VC
top_moves.inference: He is not a finance-track VC — he can and will interrogate the actual architecture, and a surface-level 'AI-native' pitch won't survive his questions.
top_moves.move: Open with how the agents are actually built — orchestration, reasoning rigor, and the concrete reason Claude-native is a structural edge, not a label. Bring one architecture slide you'd defend to an AI researcher.

top_moves.fact: On Quantinuum: 'we invested when no one in Korea was willing to look at quantum. It was a contrarian call... vindicated in the most decisive way possible.'
top_moves.inference: He is drawn to non-consensus theses he can hold with conviction, and he prizes being early/right over crowd validation.
top_moves.move: Frame Orbt's wedge (agentic country-by-country strategy + ad creative for Korean F&B/cosmetics via Slack) as a deliberate non-consensus bet — state plainly why most people underrate this and why you're right.

top_moves.fact: Chose to speak publicly on 'global AI trends and how startups can scale globally' at the Microsoft × KIP Synergy Club
top_moves.inference: Cross-border scaling of startups is a topic he has voluntarily put his name to — it maps almost exactly onto Orbt's core thesis.
top_moves.move: Tie the business model directly to global scaling: position Orbt as the engine that lets Korean brands scale cross-border, and ask for his read on which markets KIP sees opening.

non_obvious.insight: His entire public portfolio signal is hard-tech infrastructure — quantum (Quantinuum/QEDMA), edge AI (ENERZAi), manufacturing AI (INTERX), inference infra (NeuReality) — with zero visible consumer/applied-GTM bets. Orbt is an applied, consumer-go-to-market AI play, which sits off his usual moat-heavy thesis. Don't let that surface as a silent objection: pre-empt it by naming the technical moat (the agentic system + accumulated cross-border execution data), not just the market.

interaction_simulation.opening_30s: Skip pitch-deck warmup; open on the intelligence engine ('intelligence-without-search / decision-testing for D2C') and the cross-border product — both land directly on his stated AI + global-scaling interests.
interaction_simulation.likely_arc: Fast, technical, low-fluff exchange; he probes commercial deployment and global scalability, Sunwoo answers with proof points; rapport builds on shared rigor.
interaction_simulation.where_it_could_stall: If the conversation stays consumer-goods GTM, it drifts from his deep-tech/infra comfort zone (quantum, edge AI, inference); also a founder–VC power asymmetry can make it read as evaluation, not peer chemistry.
interaction_simulation.how_to_adapt: Anchor orbt's defensibility in the agentic-systems substrate (his deep-tech wheelhouse), mirror his 'contrarian conviction, commercially deployed' language, and bring a crisp metric or live use-case rather than narrative.
